LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK MD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION INCLUDE DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO OBTAIN INFORMED CONSENT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT DISCLOSED ALL PERTINENT INFORMATION ABOUT THE OPERATIONS AND OBTAINED PLAINTIFF'S INFORMED CONSENT. (COBBS V. GRANT (1972) 8 CAL.3D 229, 240-41.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BRUCE MCCORMACK, M.D. AND EDWARD EYSTER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: DR. EYSTER'S MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AND DR. MCCORMACK'S MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DR. EYSTER WAS DISMISSED ON SEPTEMBER 22, 2011. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DR. MCCORMACK DISCLOSED ALL PERTINENT INFORMATION ABOUT THE OPERATIONS AND OBTAINED PLAINTIFF'S INFORMED CONSENT. (SEE COBBS V. GRANT (1972) 8 CAL.3D 229). DR. MCCORMACK'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. A MOVING PARTY DOES NOT HAVE A RIGHT TO CONTINUANCE BASED ON PURPORTED NEED TO SUBMIT ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT CONTINUED THE TRIAL DATE FROM 12/05/11 TO 05/29/12. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK MD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Liberally construed, Plaintiff's first and second causes of action include Defendant's alleged failure to obtain informed consent. There is a triable issue whether Defendant disclosed all pertinent information about the operations and obtained Plaintiff's informed consent. (Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal.3d 229, 240-41.) Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-04-2011 Continued To May-29-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS BRUCE MCCORMACK, M.D. AND EDWARD EYSTER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Stephen Kent Rose, Esq. 332-6000 for pltf & opposing party; Claudia Lozano, Esq. of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer 397-2800 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Dr. Eyster's motion is off calendar and Dr. McCormack's motion is denied without prejudice. Dr. Eyster was dismissed on September 22, 2011. There is a triable issue whether Dr. McCormack disclosed all pertinent information about the operations and obtained plaintiff's informed consent. (See Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal.3d 229). Dr. McCormack's request for a continuance is denied. A moving party does not have a right to continuance based on purported need to submit additional evidence. In addition, the Court continued the trial date from 12/05/11 to 05/29/12. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN KENT ROSE APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ELIA M. DELUCA OF THE LYNCH, GILARDI & GRUMMER, TEL. NO. 397-2800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BRUCE MCCORMACK AND EDWARD EYSTERS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS BRUCE MCCORMACK M D AND EDWARD EYSTER MD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD IN GOOD FAITH FACTS SHOWING THAT THE OPERATION TO WHICH HE GAVE CONSENT IS SUBSTANTIALLY DIFFERENT FROM THE OPERATION PERFORMED AS OPPOSED TO HIS BELIEVING THAT HE WAS NOT GIVEN A SUFFICIENT DESCRIPTION TO PERMIT HIS INFORMED CONSENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
